explanations.en: This action ranks first because it fits the city's preferred timeframe, has very strong policy support, and looks highly feasible under the current legal and socioeconomic conditions. Its main drawback is that the expected emissions reduction is limited and it does not match the city's preferred sector.

This action ranks near the top because it matches the city's preferred sector and co-benefits, while also fitting the preferred timeframe and showing strong policy and legal support. It is held back mainly because its expected emissions reduction is very limited in the current city inventory.

This action stays high in the ranking because it fits the city's preferred timeframe, has strong policy support, and aligns well with the city's preferred co-benefits. It ranks below the top two because its expected emissions reduction is very limited and it does not match the city's preferred sector.

This action is placed just below the top group because it fits the city's preferred timeframe, has strong policy support, and supports the city's preferred co-benefits. It ranks lower because its expected emissions reduction is very limited and the legal case is only conditional.

This action ranks in the middle because it fits the preferred timeframe, has strong policy support, and supports the city's preferred co-benefits. It does not rank higher because its expected emissions reduction is limited and it does not match the city's preferred sector.

This action is supported by a favorable timeline, strong policy context, and a very good fit with the city's socioeconomic context. It ranks lower than similar actions because its expected emissions reduction is limited, it does not match the city's preferred sector, and there is no legal assessment row available.

This action remains in the lower middle because it fits the city's preferred timeframe and has very strong policy and legal support. It is held back because it does not directly match a subsector with recorded city emissions and does not match the city's preferred sector.

This action ranks below the stronger matches because it fits the city's preferred sector and timeframe and has strong co-benefit support. Its main limitation is that the expected emissions reduction is very limited, and there is no legal assessment row available.

This action is kept lower because, although it fits the preferred timeframe and has strong policy support, its expected emissions reduction is very limited. It also does not match the city's preferred sector, and no legal assessment row was available.
